perturbation.original_text: (a) Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 30 days' written notice to the other party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The clause regarding the duration and termination of the agreement is modified to change the notice period required for termination from 60 days to 30 days. This change creates an in-text contradiction when juxtaposed with subsequent references to the original 60-day notice period, especially concerning registered investment companies under the 1940 Act, introducing uncertainty in enforcing the termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration and Termination. (a) Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 30 days' written notice to the other party hereto. (b) Notwithstanding the foregoing, to the extent that the Services to be provided with respect to any Account which is registered as an investment company under the 1940 Act (herein referred to as a "registered investment company") are services referred to in the definition of "investment advisor" under Section 202(a)(11) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (herein referred to as "investment advisory services"), then with respect to such Account, this Agreement: (i) shall not commence until the effective date of its approval by the board of directors or trustees ("Board") of such Account; (ii) shall continue from year to year thereafter, subject to the provisions for termination and all other terms and conditions hereof, only if such continuation shall be specifically approved at least annually by a majority of the Board, including a majority of the members of the Board who are not parties to this Agreement or interested persons of any such party (other than as members of the Board) cast in person at a meeting called for that purpose; (iii) may be terminated at any time without the payment of any penalty by the Board or by a vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities (as defined in Section 2(a)(42) of the 1940 Act) of the Account on 60 days' written notice to the Adviser; (iv) shall automatically terminate in the event of (A) its assignment (as defined in the 1940 Act) or (B) termination of the Advisory Agreement for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 60-day written notice for termination. The changed text reduces this to 30 days. However, the contradicted text explicitly states that for registered investment companies, termination requires 60 days' written notice to the Adviser. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct contradiction regarding the notice period for terminating the agreement for registered investment companies, as it introduces a 30-day notice period where a 60-day notice period is mandated in another section of the contract, leading to an inconsistency as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: Services Fee = Cost of Services x Adviser's Assets under Management Total Assets Under Management x (1 + Applicable Margin) Where: "Cost of Services" is FASC's total Operating Costs incurred in providing the applicable Category of Services during the month to all investment advisers for which FASC provides that Category of Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Services Fee = Cost of Services x Adviser's Assets under Management Total Assets Under Management x (1 + Applicable Margin) Where: "Cost of Services" is FASC's total Operating Costs incurred in providing the applicable Category of Services during the quarter to all investment advisers for which FASC provides that Category of Services.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Cost of Services" is altered to change the calculation period from 'month' to 'quarter'. This directly contradicts the initial statement in Exhibit B that "Adviser shall pay FASC a Services Fee, payable monthly in arrears", causing confusion and uncertainty in the payment schedule and calculation, potentially leading to disputes regarding the amount and frequency of payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CALCULATION AND PAYMENT OF SERVICES FEES For each Category of Services referenced in Exhibit A, Adviser shall pay FASC a Services Fee, payable monthly in arrears, determined according to the following formula: Services Fee = Cost of Services x Adviser's Assets under Management Total Assets Under Management x (1 + Applicable Margin) Where: "Cost of Services" is FASC's total Operating Costs incurred in providing the applicable Category of Services during the quarter to all investment advisers for which FASC provides that Category of Services. "Adviser's Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which the Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadvisor and which utilize the Category of Services. "Total Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which all investment advisers (including the Adviser) to which FASC provides that Category of Services act as investment adviser or subadviser and which utilize the Category of Services. "Applicable Margin" is 0.10. "Operating Costs" means all operating expenses and non-operating expenses of FASC for the cost center(s) providing the applicable Category of Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the calculation period for "Cost of Services" from 'month' to 'quarter'. The contradicted text states "Adviser shall pay FASC a Services Fee, payable monthly in arrears". By changing the calculation period from monthly to quarterly in the changed text, this creates a direct contradiction with the 'monthly in arrears' payment schedule specified in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: FASC shall create and maintain all necessary books and records in accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including but not limited to records required by Section 31(a) of the 1940 Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the Services performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by FASC for the periods and in the places required by Rule 31a-2 under the 1940 Act.
perturbation.changed_text: FASC shall create and maintain only such books and records as it deems necessary for its internal purposes, without any obligation to comply with Section 31(a) of the 1940 Act and the rules thereunder. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by FASC for a period of one year.
perturbation.explanation: This modification alters the record-keeping obligations of FASC. Initially, FASC was required to adhere to Section 31(a) of the 1940 Act and Rule 31a-2. The change removes this obligation and imposes a retention period of just one year, contradicting the original requirements. This is an in-text contradiction because the initial section specifies compliance with the 1940 Act, which includes specific retention periods that often extend beyond one year, and the modified text explicitly avoids these requirements. This contradiction introduces significant ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which record-keeping standards apply, especially in regard to legal and regulatory compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Records. FASC shall create and maintain only such books and records as it deems necessary for its internal purposes, without any obligation to comply with Section 31(a) of the 1940 Act and the rules thereunder. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by FASC for a period of one year. The books and records pertaining to any Account which are in the possession of FAS shall be the property of such Account. The Account, or its owners or authorized representatives, shall have access to such books and records at all times during FASC's normal business hours. Upon reasonable request, copies of any such books and records shall be provided promptly by FASC to the Account or the Account's owners or authorized representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text mandated adherence to Section 31(a) of the 1940 Act and Rule 31a-2 for record keeping, which have specific retention periods. The changed text explicitly removes this obligation and imposes a retention period of just one year. Since the contradicted text is literally a copy and paste of the Changed Text, it is an in-text contradiction. The requirement to maintain records only as 'deemed necessary' and for a period of 'one year' directly conflicts with the original requirement to adhere to the 1940 Act, leading to uncertainty about which standards should be followed. This introduces a significant inconsistency regarding record-keeping standards and legal compliance.
